tasks.title: Retrieve relevant documents and policies about Booking.com no-show and cancellation disputes
Search for recent cases and advice on Booking.com no-show complaints and guest cancellations
Analyze findings to identify next steps for complaint triage and resolution
Formulate a step-by-step complaint triage plan for Booking.com no-show disputes
Deliver a compact, actionable plan for handling the Booking.com no-show complaint

rag_output.summary:
- Support must verify the timeline when a property marks a reservation as no-show after the guest claims cancellation; any timeline conflicts should be escalated [1].  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show, support should consider the charge potentially refundable and gather cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner details before responding [2].  
- Determine who collected payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, support should collect payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before proceeding [3].  

Sources: [1] refunds_policy.md, [2] refunds_policy.md, [3] payment_disputes.md

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: When the property marked the reservation as a no-show after the guest claims to have cancelled, support must verify the timeline before declining the request. Timeline conflicts should be escalated.

evidence.title: refunds_policy.md
evidence.excerpt: If the guest cancelled inside the free-cancellation window and the booking was not later marked as a no-show, support should treat the charge as potentially refundable and gather the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment owner before responding.

evidence.title: payment_disputes.md
evidence.excerpt: The first question is who collected the money: Booking.com or the property. If the property charged the guest directly, support should collect the payment receipt, property message trail, and cancellation timeline before deciding on next steps.

web_output.summary:
Task: Reporting guests as no-shows and requesting removal of guest reviews on Booking.com

Public evidence:
- Booking.com allows property partners to report guests as no-shows through their partner platform, which helps manage overbookings and guest attendance issues [1].
- Partners can request the removal of guest reviews if they violate Booking.com's content guidelines, such as containing inappropriate language or irrelevant content [2].
- The process for reporting no-shows and requesting review removals is accessible via the Booking.com partner help center, providing step-by-step instructions for partners [1][2].

Sources: [1] https://partner.booking.com/en-gb/help/reservations/overbookings-no-shows/reporting-guests-no-shows-your-property, [2] https://partner.booking.com/en-us/help/guest-reviews/general/how-request-removal-guest-review

evidence.title: Reporting guests no-shows at your property - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: You can mark the booking as a no-show. This prevents guests from automatically receiving a satisfaction survey when they didn't stay with you.

evidence.title: How to request the removal of a guest review - Partner Hub
evidence.excerpt: The reservation is marked as a no-show and the review doesn't detail the guest's experience; You canceled the booking on the planned day of ...

evidence.title: Handling guest misconduct: a guide
evidence.excerpt: On this page, you'll learn how to protect yourself, your staff and your property against guest misbehaviour and incidents.

action_plan:
- Verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation and the property’s no-show marking by collecting the cancellation timestamp, property’s no-show report time, and relevant communication to identify any conflicts; escalate if discrepancies arise.  
- Determine who processed the payment (Booking.com or property); if the property charged directly, obtain the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation details to assess refund eligibility according to the free-cancellation policy.  
- Advise the property on the appropriate steps to report no-shows or request guest review removals via the Booking.com partner platform, referencing official guidelines to ensure compliance and proper handling.
next_steps:
1. Collect and verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp, the property’s no-show marking time, and all relevant communication to identify any timeline discrepancies.  
2. Confirm who processed the payment—Booking.com or the property—and if the property charged directly, request the payment receipt and message trail to evaluate refund eligibility.  
3. If timeline conflicts or policy violations are found, escalate the case to the legal or refunds team for further review.  
4. Advise the property on using the Booking.com partner platform to report no-shows properly and guide them on requesting guest review removals according to official guidelines.
final_answer:
Issue:  
A property has marked a booking as a no-show after the guest claims they cancelled the reservation. There is a need to verify the accuracy of these claims and determine the appropriate refund or dispute resolution steps.

Internal policy:  
- Support must verify the timeline of the guest’s cancellation versus the property’s no-show marking; any conflicts should be escalated for further review.  
- If the guest cancelled within the free-cancellation window and the booking was not marked no-show at that time, the charge may be refundable. Support should collect the cancellation timestamp, property policy, and payment details before responding.  
- It is essential to determine who collected payment (Booking.com or the property). If the property charged directly, support must obtain the payment receipt, message trail, and cancellation timeline to assess refund eligibility.

Public policy:  
- Booking.com allows properties to report guests as no-shows through their partner platform to manage attendance issues.  
- Properties can request removal of guest reviews that violate content guidelines via the Booking.com partner help center.  
- Clear procedures and step-by-step instructions are available to partners for reporting no-shows and requesting review removals.

Recommended action:  
- Collect and verify the guest’s cancellation timestamp, the property’s no-show marking time, and all relevant communications to identify any timeline discrepancies.  
- Confirm who processed the payment and, if the property charged directly, request payment receipts and message trails to evaluate refund eligibility.  
- If discrepancies or policy violations are found, escalate the case to the legal or refunds team.  
- Advise the property to use the Booking.com partner platform for reporting no-shows and guide them on requesting guest review removals per official guidelines.

Next steps:  
1. Request and review the guest’s cancellation timestamp, property’s no-show report time, and communication history.  
2. Determine the payment processor and, if applicable, collect payment receipts and message trails from the property.  
3. Escalate the case if timeline conflicts or policy issues arise.  
4. Provide the property with instructions and links to the Booking.com partner help center resources for proper no-show reporting and guest review removal requests.
